translations: Since it was a story where dreams came true, I'm also...
I'm looking forward to looking back on it with you all today.
Also, AfterTalk has had a bit of a mini-renewal!
We collected messages beforehand via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
I remember performing my lines with such a bright
and happy heart.
It was a story where I really felt Shiho-chan's significance.
Thank you all so much for the messages.
I'll be reading some of them during the show, so please stay tuned!
Also, please post your thoughts on the event or this stream using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat too,
so I'll be picking up any comments that catch my eye.
I see so many "{\i1}Kon-needo{\i0}s" in the chat!
Thank you so much.
Someone just commented, "I just finished reading the story." Thank you!
Thank you very much.
Well then, let's dive right in.
I'd like to start by talking about the story for "Heartbeat That Connects with You."
How did you all find the event this time?
I'll look back on the story while reading through your letters.
First, we have a letter from Misora-san.

From: Misora-san
Noguchi-san, {\i1}Kon-needo{\i0}!
{\i1}Kon-needo{\i0}!
Thank you for your wonderful voice as always.
No, thank you!
While reading this event story,
I was so moved and heart-warmed by how Ichika-chan
put her strong feelings for Miku-chan into a song.
Also, Shiho-chan's mysterious vibe at the end really piqued my interest.
Regardless, seeing Ichika-chan so happy made me feel happy too.
Misora-san, thanks for the message!
Yeah, this time Ichika wrote a song about what Miku means to her.
It's called "With," and it's packed with everything
from Miku and Ichika's journey together.
After she uploaded it, a creator named Aoi-san reached out to make an MV for it.
It got so many views
and reached so many people in various ways.
Like I said at the beginning, it was a story so full of joy
and happiness that I almost wondered
if it was okay to be this happy. It really was
a lovely story.
Ichika's goal of connecting people through music has been achieved.
It's only the first step, but since her wish came true,
I remember feeling very
refreshed and happy while recording my lines.
You also mentioned being curious about Shiho-chan's mysterious vibe.
What do you all think?
How did it make you feel?
Shiho's last words were about how Ichika looked like she was having a lot of fun,
but... for me...
Well, yeah, I'm sure there's a bit of a conflicted feeling...
I don't think Shiho is quite at that "conflicted" stage yet,
though.
It's probably more like she's wondering, "What is this feeling?"
We were accepting messages for the AfterTalk.
But in the midst of that, Ichika is, well, on her own...
writing a song for Miku-san, and...
I think she must have felt something through that.
As for...
what kind of story it's going to become from here on, I have no idea.
I wonder if that's just how bands are? I'm not really sure.
But you know, there are plenty of people who write songs with Miku-san
while also being in a band themselves,
right?
So I hope Ichika can find a good balance
and keep doing things that make her happy.
I want her to keep doing her best as a member of Leo/need, too.
And I want her to keep writing songs with her beloved Miku-san.
I don't know what the future holds, but I hope we can all watch over her warmly.
I really do.
Right.
Now then, I'd like to read another letter.
This one is from YMNK-san.
"Noguchi-san, {i1}kon-needo{i0}."
{i1}Kon-needo{i0}!
In this event story, Ichika created the song "With,"
pouring her feelings for herself and Miku into it.
There was a scene where she seemed satisfied just having finished it and letting everyone hear it, but...
Noguchi-san,
have you ever felt satisfied just by finishing a certain task?
That's the message.
Thanks for the letter!
Right, Saki-chan suggested she should upload it since she went to the trouble of writing it, so she did.
Hmm, something I felt satisfied just by doing?
Let's see...
Recently, I made some curry.
I suddenly felt like making curry, but once I was done, I was satisfied...
and then eating it felt like a chore.
I made enough to last for several days.
I really did, everyone, but I just felt so satisfied once it was finished.
Exactly like the story.
I was satisfied just making it. Plus, food you cook yourself doesn't always seem that appetizing...
I mean,
I ate it like normal, but I guess I just felt satisfied once the cooking was over.
Also, this is totally unrelated, but...
I didn't have a kitchen knife at home. It was just gone.
I don't know why.
I definitely had one before.
I had one, but it suddenly vanished.
Isn't that scary?
So, I wanted to cut some carrots,
but I couldn't, so I used scissors to snip them—
just like that,
and that's the whole story.
Yeah.
Was I satisfied with that?
Yeah.
I put so much energy
into making it that I was satisfied, and I didn't have the strength left to eat it...
or so I thought.
I was like, "No way!"
I did eat it later, so please don't worry.
What am I even talking about?
Seriously.
Anyway, thank you for the question.
Right.
Anyway, moving on... I've picked out some scenes I personally want to talk about,
so let's watch them
together.
Here's the video.
Okay, I've picked out Episode 8 for today.
3 million views...
Isn't that amazing?
It's great that so many people have been listening to it so steadily.
It really is.
That's true.
I think it's because so many people helped connect us to this point.
Honestly, I didn't think it would happen this fast.
I feel the same way.
Maybe 5 million is next?
It'd be nice to reach that milestone.
Yeah.
We'll have to start listening together once it hits 4.95 million.
Waiting for those last 50,000 views sounds like quite the task.
I love how KAITO-san muttered that. With their current momentum...
Right, and coming in 2nd place was a huge deal.
Haruka-chan mentioned
at the start how people on the internet
were making predictions like that.
She was wondering what rank they'd actually end up with.
It felt so realistic.
Momojan actually looks at
the online votes and predictions too.
They use that information as a reference for their future self-production.
Taking every hint they can get and then
deciding what to do next... it's so mature.
I mean, they're only high schoolers.
I don't think most high schoolers could do all that.
Anyway, thank you so much for all the messages.
And thank you too, Yukkii-san.
Um, those were the only two letters, but...
I have so much I want to say,
so please let me take my time
and talk about it.
You know,
the Lumina Grand Prix... for the players,
for ProSeka players, it felt like it took
a really long time,
didn't it?
It was released bit by bit, and I kept wondering when the qualifiers would ever end.
Part of me wanted to see what happened next right away, but
another part of me wished it would just never end.
Right when I was caught between those feelings, we finally moved from the qualifiers to the finals.
I was actually incredibly nervous at that moment.
And on top of that, when I realized it was Haruka's banner, I was like,
"Wait, this story is going to be told from Haruka's perspective?"
Especially in Haruka's case—and I'll talk about this more later—
there's this very clear wall in the form of Hiiragi-san and Rewrite,
right?
She's someone Haruka can't help but be conscious of.
And now she has to face that again.
Last time she "fought" Hiiragi-san—if that's the right word—
when they faced off and talked one-on-one, it was...
It depicted a sort of battle against the adults in the real industry,
and Haruka's expression was so dark back then.
